title: Farsellah

''The Essential Atlas'' also places the portion of the Expansion Region north of the Perlemian within the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]].</ref>

{{Quote|There have been several sightings of Baem on many other worlds, including Endovan, Farsellah, and Kluistar, most of which are under the control of the New Republic.|Personal addendum by Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
'''Farsellah''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="WbC" /> located in the [[Farsellah system/Legends|Farsellah system]], a part of the [[Farstey sector/Legends|Farstey sector]] in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="Trans-Hydian" />

By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Wanted by Cracken'' translate to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] informant [[Rodin Baem]] was sighted on many worlds, most of which were controlled by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Those worlds included Farsellah, and in a personal addendum to an entry on Baem included in the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] noted that Baem's appearance on those worlds potentially signaled that the Empire would soon attack them and that the respective [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] should make appropriate preparations.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Farsellah was mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], which was written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Farsellah system, and therefore the world Farsellah, in grid square O-8.<ref name="TEA Farsellah" />
